---
title: "Diagnostics"
---

## Per-Site R2 Distribution (CDF)

Empirical cumulative distribution of per-site R2 at zero-shot, N=5, and N=10 adaptation. The vertical line marks R2=0 — sites to the left are worse than predicting the mean.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-r2-cdf
from pathlib import Path
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")
from _scripts.figures import make_per_site_r2_cdf

fig = make_per_site_r2_cdf(Path("dashboard_data/per_site.parquet"))
fig.show()
```

## Residual Diagnostics

Four views of model residuals: vs predicted value (heteroscedasticity), Q-Q plot (normality), vs turbidity (input dependence), and histogram with normal overlay. The heavy tails (skew and kurtosis annotated) justify the Student-t prior in the Bayesian adaptation.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-residual-diagnostics
from _scripts.figures import make_residual_diagnostics

fig = make_residual_diagnostics(Path("dashboard_data/per_reading.parquet"))
fig.show()
```

## Temporal Stability

For sites with time-stamped data, each site's readings are split chronologically into first half and second half. R2 is computed independently for each half. Sites near the diagonal are temporally stable; sites far from it may reflect sensor drift, land use change, or non-stationarity.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-temporal-stability
from _scripts.figures import make_temporal_stability

fig = make_temporal_stability(Path("dashboard_data/per_reading.parquet"))
fig.show()
```

## Data Funnel

How the dataset was built: from 860 discovered USGS sites down to the final 254 training + 76 holdout + 36 vault split. Each narrowing step filters sites that lack turbidity sensors, temporal overlap, or watershed attributes.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-data-sankey
from _scripts.figures import make_data_sankey

fig = make_data_sankey(Path("dashboard_data/summary.json"))
fig.show()
```

## SSC vs Turbidity Joint Distribution

The raw data the model learns from: SSC plotted against turbidity across all holdout readings.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-joint-distribution
from _scripts.figures import make_joint_distribution

fig = make_joint_distribution(Path("dashboard_data/per_reading.parquet"))
fig.show()
```

## Box-Cox Transform Effect

Why Box-Cox lambda=0.2? Comparison of the SSC distribution under three transforms. The Box-Cox transform reduces skewness while preserving more of the extreme-value structure than log1p.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-boxcox-comparison
from _scripts.figures import make_boxcox_comparison

fig = make_boxcox_comparison(Path("dashboard_data/per_reading.parquet"))
fig.show()
```

## Power-Law Slope Distribution

Distribution of per-site turbidity-SSC slopes in log-log space. Slopes near 1.0 indicate a linear relationship; slopes < 1 mean SSC saturates at high turbidity (coarse material); slopes > 1 mean SSC accelerates (fine material mobilization).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-slope-gallery
from _scripts.figures import make_slope_gallery

fig = make_slope_gallery(Path("dashboard_data/per_reading.parquet"))
fig.show()
```

## Rating Curves

Six holdout sites spanning the performance range, showing the raw turbidity-SSC relationship. Gray dots are observed; colored dots are model predictions.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-rating-curves
from _scripts.figures import make_rating_curves

fig = make_rating_curves(
    Path("dashboard_data/per_reading.parquet"),
    Path("dashboard_data/per_site.parquet"),
)
fig.show()
```

## Feature Importance

Top features ranked by importance. Colors indicate feature category.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-feature-importance
from _scripts.figures import make_shap_beeswarm

fig = make_shap_beeswarm()
fig.show()
```

## Load Estimation

Cumulative sediment load comparison for selected holdout sites. If the curves track closely, the model produces accurate total loads even when individual predictions scatter.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-load-estimation
from _scripts.figures import make_load_estimation

fig = make_load_estimation(Path("dashboard_data/per_reading.parquet"))
fig.show()
```

## Sediment Load by Timescale

Discharge-weighted sediment load (SSC × Q × 0.0027 tons/day) compared at daily, monthly, and annual aggregation. Even if individual predictions scatter, loads should converge at longer timescales if errors are unbiased.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-load-timescale
from _scripts.figures import make_sediment_load_timescale

fig = make_sediment_load_timescale(Path("dashboard_data/per_reading.parquet"))
fig.show()
```
